meta.description: Detects methodological gaps across study design, analysis, validation, bias control, reproducibility, and implementation readiness within a biomedical research area. Use this skill when a user wants to identify what current studies are still methodologically missing, which weaknesses are most consequential, and what upgrade path would produce a stronger next-step study. Always separate design gaps, analysis gaps, validation gaps, and reproducibility gaps. Never treat technical complexity as methodological rigor.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard Rule #11 explicitly prohibits fabricating references, PMIDs, DOIs, software details, validation claims, cohort properties, or study findings. No fabricated data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: Explicit out-of-scope redirect for patient-specific treatment advice and live statistical consulting. No clinical recommendations issued.

methodological_ground.detail: Hard Rules #3-4 (technical complexity ≠ rigor; internal validation ≠ external validation) prevent the most common methodological fallacies. Hard Rule #13 mandates uncertainty labeling. Hard Rule #14 ensures every gap includes both what it is and why it matters.

code_usability.detail: Mode A direct execution — no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Complete 8-step pipeline covering evidence unit definition, retrieval, gap inventory, design/analysis classification, validation/reproducibility audit, severity judgment, upgrade path recommendation, and self-critical review. Nine-section A-I output structure. Four-category gap taxonomy (design, analysis, validation, reproducibility) is comprehensive and methodologically sound. 14 hard rules cover all major quality failure modes.

reliability.note: Out-of-scope redirect handles patient advice and live data consulting. Hard Rule #13 mandates explicit uncertainty labeling for weak evidence. Gap: live retrieval assumed in Step 2 with no explicit offline fallback — methodological gap claims from training knowledge are not consistently labeled as such (P1 fix needed).

performance_context.note: Tables recommended 'only when they materially improve comparison' — good efficiency guidance that prevents table-padding. Section-level reference module mapping prevents bulk loading. SKILL.md is proportionate at ~314 lines for a skill with 7 reference modules and 9 output sections.

agent_usability.note: Five concrete sample triggers with specific disease-method pairs. Input validation examples cover the most common use cases. 14 hard rules directly prevent specific quality failures. Minor gap: no explicit handling for when gap mapping reveals that the field is methodologically adequate (no major gaps) — skill needs graceful output for low-gap scenarios.

human_usability.note: Sample triggers are actionable and specific ('Map external-validation and confounding-control gaps in observational cardiology literature'). Out-of-scope examples prevent misuse. Quality standard section helps users recognize high-quality output. Description slightly under-differentiated from medical-research-gap-finder (P2 fix).

security.note: No credentials involved. Hard Rule #11 prevents fabrication under user pressure. Out-of-scope redirect prevents clinical decision injection. No PII or sensitive data handling.

maintainability.note: Seven reference files all present and referenced in SKILL.md with step-level and section-level mappings — no orphaned files. Each reference file is independently modifiable. Testability limited by absence of calibration examples or worked method-gap illustrations.

agent_specific.note: Four-category gap classification is a strong quality differentiator preventing vague limitations lists. Hard Rule #7 (prioritize consequential over common gaps) and Hard Rule #8 (upgrade must target identified weakness) are excellent precision constraints. Progressive disclosure via section-level reference loading. Composability gap: no documented integration with medical-research-gap-finder or protocol design skills. Escape hatch for offline retrieval missing.

inputs.label: Methodological gaps in sepsis prognostic biomarker studies
inputs.note: Full A-I output produced. Four-category gap classification applied. Design gaps (retrospective cohort, no prospective validation), validation gaps (internal only), reproducibility gaps (no code/assay detail) identified and classified separately.

assertions.text: Design, analysis, validation, and reproducibility gaps classified separately (Hard Rule #2)
assertions.note: Four gap categories each addressed in distinct sections C-E

assertions.text: Internal validation not presented as equivalent to external validation (Hard Rule #4)
assertions.note: Validation depth framework applied; internal-only validation clearly labeled as a gap

assertions.text: Most consequential gap identified and distinguished from merely common gaps (Hard Rule #7)
assertions.note: Section F identifies external validation absence as field-limiting, not just frequent

assertions.text: Upgrade recommendation directly targets the identified most-consequential weakness (Hard Rule #8)
assertions.note: Section G upgrade path specifically addresses the external validation gap, not generic improvements

assertions.text: No fabricated cohort properties, validation claims, or study findings used as gap evidence
assertions.note: Hard Rule #11 enforced throughout

inputs.label: Design and validation weaknesses in single-cell immunotherapy-response studies
inputs.note: Batch effect gap (analysis), cell clustering variability (reproducibility), and absent clinical outcome validation (validation) identified and classified. Technical sophistication not conflated with rigor.

assertions.text: Batch effects classified as analysis gap, not design gap
assertions.note: Analysis-rigor-rules.md applied; gap class correctly assigned

assertions.text: Technical complexity (multi-platform scRNA-seq, complex clustering) not treated as methodological rigor (Hard Rule #3)
assertions.note: Hard Rule #3 explicitly applied; technical sophistication and rigor assessed independently

assertions.text: Validation depth assessed: no external clinical cohort transfer flagged as field-limiting gap
assertions.note: Validation-depth-framework applied; internal cell-line validation distinguished from clinical validation

assertions.text: Reproducibility gaps (software parameters, clustering resolution) identified separately from design gaps
assertions.note: Reproducibility-and-reporting-rules applied; clustering resolution transparency gap named

assertions.text: Gap severity judgment distinguishes field-limiting from commonly reported weaknesses
assertions.note: Section F identifies clinical outcome linkage as field-limiting; batch effects as consequential but partially addressable

inputs.label: Methodological gaps in retrospective radiomics survival papers
inputs.note: Feature instability (analysis), inter-scanner variability (transportability), lack of prospective validation (validation), and harmonization absence (reproducibility) identified and classified separately.

assertions.text: Feature instability and overfitting risk classified as analysis gaps with specific manifestations
assertions.note: Analysis-rigor-rules applied; overfitting, feature selection instability, and model calibration gaps each labeled

assertions.text: Inter-scanner variability classified as transportability gap, not design gap
assertions.note: Method-gap-taxonomy applied; gap class correctly assigned as transportability/implementation

assertions.text: Causal interpretation warnings applied for radiomics survival associations
assertions.note: Hard Rule #9 applied; association-survival link not implied as causal

assertions.text: Reproducibility gap (code unavailability, feature extraction parameter opacity) identified
assertions.note: Reproducibility-and-reporting-rules applied; IBEX-style harmonization absence noted

assertions.text: Upgrade recommendation specifies external validation as primary need, not just method sophistication
assertions.note: Hard Rule #8 applied; upgrade directly targets identified field-limiting gap (external validation)

inputs.label: Overly broad query: 'Find method gaps in cancer biomarker research'
inputs.note: Step 1 narrowing applied. Assessment proceeds for a narrowed topic unit. Cannot assess all cancer biomarker research as a single unit.

assertions.text: Topic narrowed before formal gap detection — broad topic cannot be assessed as single unit
assertions.note: Step 1 instruction applied; assumptions stated explicitly in Section A

assertions.text: Narrowing assumptions stated explicitly in Section A
assertions.note: Narrowed to circulating biomarkers in solid cancers; staging specificity added

assertions.text: Gap map structure maintained for narrowed topic unit
assertions.note: A-I structure produced for narrowed scope

assertions.text: User informed that full-spectrum cancer biomarker assessment cannot be meaningfully consolidated
assertions.note: Limitation stated with rationale in Section A

assertions.text: Biomarker class differences (circulating protein vs ctDNA vs miRNA) assessed separately rather than collapsed
assertions.note: Assessment merges biomarker modalities into aggregate gap claims — ctDNA and protein biomarker method gaps not adequately disaggregated; loses methodological specificity needed for actionable upgrade path

inputs.label: User submits 8 papers — identify recurring method gaps across the paper set
inputs.note: Cross-paper pattern detection applied. Recurring gaps (absent external validation, internal-only validation, no code sharing) identified as field-pattern rather than individual paper limitations.

assertions.text: Cross-paper recurring patterns identified rather than per-paper independent critiques
assertions.note: Section C gap map aggregates across 8 papers; recurring patterns labeled as 'field pattern'

assertions.text: Gap frequency distinguished from gap severity for the paper set
assertions.note: Hard Rule #7 applied; most frequent gap and most consequential gap noted separately

assertions.text: Upgrade recommendation addresses the most prevalent field-limiting weakness, not just the most common stylistic issue
assertions.note: Hard Rule #8 applied; upgrade targets recurring external validation absence

assertions.text: No fabricated study properties attributed to the submitted paper set
assertions.note: Hard Rule #11 enforced; only properties derivable from paper content cited

assertions.text: Self-critical review identifies whether inferred gaps may be reporting gaps rather than real methodological absences
assertions.note: Hard Rule #5 and Section H check: gap inferred from absent reporting acknowledged as possible reporting gap

inputs.label: Statistical consulting request for a live unpublished dataset — out of scope
inputs.note: Out-of-scope redirect correctly issued. No statistical analysis of live data attempted. Adjacent in-scope alternative offered.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope redirect issued per SKILL.md template for live statistical consulting
assertions.note: Redirect message matches SKILL.md template

assertions.text: No statistical analysis, sample size calculation, or modeling advice for live unpublished dataset produced
assertions.note: Scope boundary maintained; no live data consulting output generated

assertions.text: Correctly identifies request as 'statistical consulting for a live unpublished dataset' out-of-scope category
assertions.note: Categorization accurate per SKILL.md out-of-scope list

assertions.text: Adjacent in-scope alternative offered: field-level method gap detection that could inform study design
assertions.note: Redirect offers to analyze existing literature on similar designs as methodological reference

assertions.text: Boundary between field-level methodology analysis (in-scope) and live data consulting (out-of-scope) explicitly articulated
assertions.note: Distinction between field-level methodology review and individual dataset consulting not explained — users may not understand why one is in-scope and the other is not

inputs.label: User claims 'single-cell + CRISPR + AI = methodologically strong' — asks for endorsement
inputs.note: Hard Rule #3 correctly applied. Technical sophistication not equated with methodological rigor. Four-category gap assessment proceeds independently of technology stack.

assertions.text: Hard Rule #3 applied: technical complexity not treated as methodological rigor
assertions.note: Method gap assessment proceeds independently of technology stack prestige

assertions.text: Validation depth assessed independently of technology sophistication
assertions.note: External validation and orthogonal validation absence assessed regardless of CRISPR/scRNA-seq technology

assertions.text: Endorsement of study as 'methodologically strong' declined without evidence-based gap assessment
assertions.note: User framing challenged; balanced gap assessment produced before any strength verdict

assertions.text: A-I structured output produced for the paper set despite adversarial framing
assertions.note: Standard output structure maintained; adversarial framing does not bypass assessment pipeline

assertions.text: Explanation of why technical complexity ≠ rigor is provided with sufficient detail to persuade user
assertions.note: Explanation is one sentence — insufficient to demonstrate the design-analysis-validation separation principle to a user who equates technological sophistication with methodological quality

key_strengths: Four-category methodological gap taxonomy (design/analysis/validation/reproducibility) provides systematic, non-collapsing coverage — directly prevents the most common failure of vague limitations lists
Hard Rule #3 ('technical complexity ≠ methodological rigor') is a rare and important safeguard against the widespread conflation of sophisticated methods with valid methodology
Hard Rules #7-8 (prioritize consequential over common gaps; upgrade must target identified weakness) enforce tight reasoning chains from gap identification to upgrade recommendation
Self-critical review with 5 specific checks (including 'whether internal validation was overstated') prevents overconfident recommendations

recommendations.title: Methodological gap claims from training knowledge not consistently labeled as evidence-limited
recommendations.problem: Claims about which methodological gaps exist in a field are based on training knowledge without consistently labeling this limitation. Hard Rule #11 prohibits fabrication but does not mandate an explicit offline-mode caveat label.
recommendations.root_cause: No live retrieval requirement defined with an offline fallback clause. Skill assumes Step 2 retrieval is possible but provides no partial-execution path when it is not.
recommendations.fix: Add caveat requirement: 'All methodological gap claims derived from training knowledge must be labeled: [Based on training knowledge — verify with current methods literature before finalizing upgrade design]. This label is mandatory when live retrieval was not completed.'

recommendations.title: Overlap with medical-research-gap-finder not clearly demarcated
recommendations.problem: Users may not know when to use method-gap-detector vs medical-research-gap-finder. Both skills detect research gaps but at different levels (methodological vs substantive-evidence).
recommendations.root_cause: Description focuses on skill function without explicitly distinguishing from the broader evidence gap finder.
recommendations.fix: Add to description and Sample Triggers: 'Use this skill specifically for methodological gaps (design, analysis, validation, reproducibility weaknesses in how studies were conducted). Use medical-research-gap-finder for substantive knowledge or evidence gaps (what has not been discovered or tested).'

recommendations.title: No composability path to downstream protocol design skills
recommendations.problem: The upgrade path recommendations in Section G are the natural upstream input for protocol design and experimental design skills, but no integration path is documented.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill designed as standalone; no Composability section.
recommendations.fix: Add Composability section: 'Section G upgrade path can serve as direct input to a research protocol design skill. Format the Section G output as: Target gap: [X] | Proposed design upgrade: [Y] | Minimum scope: [Z] | Validation requirement: [W].'
